refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated performance requirement derivation, recording, and management system that translates worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints) into documented performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins), maintains a complete audit trail of baseline establishment and calibration updates, and ensures traceability from worksite inputs through performance targets to system design parameters.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit mention of traceability, audit trail, and the bidirectional linkage between worksite context and performance targets. Refinement clarifies that this is a closed-loop system requirement with documented evidence of derivation and change management, critical for safety-critical systems and regulatory compliance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) worksite mission context is captured in a structured format; (2) performance targets are derived via documented methodology; (3) traceability matrix links worksite inputs → performance targets → design parameters; (4) baseline and calibration history is immutable and timestamped; (5) acceptance criteria are met for at least one representative worksite scenario.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Capture and structure worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive performance targets from mission context using documented methodology.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and maintain traceability from worksite inputs to performance targets.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain immutable audit trail of baseline establishment and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide query and reporting interface for performance target history and traceability.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance requirement management database with structured worksite context schema, derivation rule engine, and immutable audit log; integrated with telemetry and tasking interfaces.
DP-B: Distributed performance target calculation across vehicle and teleoperation modules with eventual consistency synchronization and local audit trails.
DP-C: Manual performance target specification by site manager with post-hoc documentation and change log; minimal automation.
DP-D: Hybrid approach: centralized requirement repository with federated derivation logic in vehicle and teleoperation modules, synchronized via network link with conflict resolution.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance requirement management database with structured worksite context schema, derivation rule engine, and immutable audit log; integrated with telemetry and tasking interfaces.

potential_sources: Coupling to Telemetry interface: performance targets must be validated against real-time telemetry; mitigation via asynchronous event-driven updates.
Coupling to Tasking interface: performance targets drive task scheduling and resource allocation; mitigation via clear contract definition.
Coupling to Safety Services: safety-critical performance targets (e.g., availability, safety margins) must be coordinated; mitigation via explicit safety constraint input.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling to functional requirements because it centralizes the derivation logic and audit trail, reducing distributed state and synchronization complexity. Coupling to external interfaces is manageable via well-defined contracts. DP-B would exhibit higher coupling due to distributed derivation and eventual consistency issues. DP-C would fail to meet traceability and audit trail requirements.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured schema; risk: schema may be incomplete or evolve, requiring versioning.
Assumption: Derivation rules are deterministic and can be formally specified; risk: domain expertise may be tacit, requiring iterative refinement.
Assumption: Network link is reliable for database synchronization; risk: offline operation or network partitions may cause stale data; mitigation: local caching with conflict resolution.

Assumption: Safety Services module can provide safety constraints as inputs; risk: safety constraints may be dynamic or context-dependent; mitigation: real-time constraint updates via SafetyAlert interface.
Risk: High safety criticality requires formal verification of derivation rules and audit trail integrity; evidence needed: formal specification and V&V plan.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the derivation rule engine specification: define the formal language for rules, the process for rule validation and approval, and the mechanism for rule versioning and rollback. Establish explicit contracts with Safety Services for safety constraint inputs and with Telemetry/Tasking for performance target validation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the centralized performance requirement management system, including the worksite context schema, derivation rule engine, requirement database, and audit log. It serves as the single source of truth for performance targets and their traceability.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to validate performance targets against actual system behavior and inform baseline calibration.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance targets and task constraints to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to guide task scheduling and resource allocation.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services module; outputs safety-critical performance targets and constraint violations for real-time safety management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Synchronizes performance requirement database and audit log across distributed NGHE Solution instances (e.g., site manager console, vehicle onboard, tele-operator station).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of the centralized performance requirement management system. The requirement derivation, recording, and management logic is cohesive and belongs at the system level. Minimal diffusion: only external interface contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

Vehicle module could host local performance target caching and validation, but the authoritative derivation and audit trail must remain centralized. High diffusion risk: duplicating derivation logic across vehicle instances would violate single source of truth principle and complicate audit trail management.

Teleoperation module could provide user interface for performance target specification, but the underlying derivation engine and audit log must be centralized. High diffusion risk: similar to Vehicle, distributed derivation would compromise traceability and safety criticality.

Safety Services could own safety-specific performance targets (e.g., safety margins, availability), but the overall requirement management system must be unified. Partial containment: Safety Services provides constraint inputs and validates safety-critical targets, but does not own the derivation engine or audit trail.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the optimal owner because: (1) it is the system-level integrator responsible for end-to-end performance management; (2) centralizing the derivation engine and audit log ensures single source of truth and traceability; (3) it naturally interfaces with all other modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) via well-defined contracts; (4) it supports the acceptance criteria of documented performance targets and audit trail.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a bottleneck if performance requirement derivation is computationally intensive or latency-sensitive; mitigation: profile derivation performance and consider caching or asynchronous processing.
Risk: Centralized database may be a single point of failure; mitigation: implement redundancy, replication, and failover mechanisms via Network Link.
Risk: Worksite context schema may be difficult to standardize across diverse worksites; mitigation: design schema with extensibility and versioning support.
Risk: Safety Services module may have conflicting or incomplete constraint inputs; mitigation: establish formal process for constraint validation and conflict resolution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define explicit ownership boundaries and interface contracts: (1) NGHE Solution owns the centralized requirement database, derivation engine, and audit log; (2) Vehicle and Teleoperation modules own local performance target caching and validation; (3) Safety Services module owns safety constraint specification and validation; (4) all modules synchronize via Network Link with conflict resolution rules. Establish a change control board for derivation rule updates and safety constraint changes.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (centralized performance requirement management database with derivation rule engine and immutable audit log) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR: it provides a method to derive performance targets from worksite context, records them in a structured database, manages their lifecycle, and maintains traceability and audit trail. The DP is logically sound and feasible.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: If derivation logic is duplicated across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, audit trail integrity and traceability will be compromised. Mitigation: enforce centralized ownership in NGHE Solution module.

Diffusion risk: If worksite context schema is not standardized, different modules may interpret requirements differently. Mitigation: define schema in NGHE Solution module and enforce via interface contracts.

Diffusion risk: If performance target validation is distributed, conflicts may arise between local and centralized targets. Mitigation: implement synchronization and conflict resolution via Network Link.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module provides excellent containment for the centralized requirement management system. The module boundary is clear: NGHE Solution owns the database, derivation engine, and audit log; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules consume performance targets via well-defined interfaces. No significant containment risks identified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) worksite context capture can be verified via schema validation and test scenarios; (2) performance target derivation can be verified via rule testing and traceability matrix review; (3) audit trail can be verified via log inspection and immutability checks; (4) acceptance criteria can be verified via integration testing with representative worksite scenarios.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution module is responsible for the entire CR-NGHE-005 requirement. Sub-requirements (CR-NGHE-005.1 through 005.5) are allocated to specific components within NGHE Solution (e.g., context capture module, derivation engine, database, audit log, reporting interface). External dependencies on Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules are explicit via interface contracts.

recommended_revisions: Establish formal specification of the worksite context schema, including data types, constraints, and versioning strategy.
Define the derivation rule language and provide examples of rules for common performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability).
Specify the audit log format, immutability mechanism (e.g., cryptographic hashing), and retention policy.
Define interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link, including data formats, timing, and error handling.
Establish a change control process for derivation rules and safety constraints, including approval authority and rollback procedures.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-defined and the selected DP is sound, but the requirement is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through 005.5) represent distinct functional concerns that must be addressed at the next level of decomposition. Proceeding to the next level will clarify the design of the worksite context schema, derivation rule engine, database, audit log, and reporting interface.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Capture and structure worksite mission context (site conditions, operational policies, safety constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive performance targets from mission context using documented methodology.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and maintain traceability from worksite inputs to performance targets.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain immutable audit trail of baseline establishment and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide query and reporting interface for performance target history and traceability.

assumptions: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured schema with reasonable completeness.
Derivation rules can be formally specified and validated.
Network Link is reliable for database synchronization, with local caching for offline operation.
Safety Services module can provide safety constraints as inputs to the derivation engine.
Telemetry and Tasking interfaces provide sufficient data for performance target validation and task scheduling.
NGHE Solution module has sufficient computational and storage capacity for centralized requirement management.
open_risks: High safety criticality requires formal verification of derivation rules and audit trail integrity; V&V plan needed.
Worksite context schema may evolve; versioning and migration strategy needed.
Derivation rule engine may become a bottleneck; performance profiling and optimization needed.
Centralized database may be a single point of failure; redundancy and failover mechanisms needed.
Safety constraint conflicts may arise; conflict resolution process needed.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of worksite context schema with examples from representative worksites.
Derivation rule language specification with examples of rules for common performance targets.
Audit log format specification with immutability mechanism and retention policy.
Interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link.
Change control process for derivation rules and safety constraints.
V&V plan for derivation rules and audit trail integrity.
Performance profiling results for derivation engine.
Redundancy and failover design for centralized database.
